value.text:
Here are some of the biggest AI headlines circulating today, with source links:

---

## 1. OpenAI reportedly developing a social network around ChatGPT
OpenAI is said to be working on a social platform featuring a feed and image-sharing powered by ChatGPT. It’s reportedly still in internal testing.  
**Sources:** Reuters, The Verge, Business Insider  
- Reuters: “OpenAI developing social media network…” ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/business/media-telecom/openai-developing-social-media-network-information-reports-2025-04-15/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

---

## 2. Meta releases Llama 4 “Scout” and “Maverick”
Meta announced the launch of the Llama 4 family, including Scout and Maverick, calling it their most advanced open models yet.  
**Sources:** Meta newsroom, Reuters, AP  
- Meta newsroom: “Introducing Llama 4” ([about.fb.com](https://about.fb.com/news/2025/04/introducing-llama-4-scout-maverick-and-behemoth/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))  
- Reuters coverage via AP syndication ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/meta-ai-llama-chatbot-facebook-instagram-whatsapp-8216c80ca4ca1629f11479e968bb3d3f?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

---

## 3. Microsoft AI CEO says memory will be key to assistant usefulness
Microsoft AI chief Mustafa Suleyman said at a recent event that improving AI memory—so assistants remember who you are and your preferences—is critical to making them more useful.  
**Sources:** Reuters, company remarks  
- Reuters: coverage of Suleyman’s comments ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/memory-about-users-will-make-ai-assistants-more-helpful-says-microsoft-ai-ceo-2025-04-04/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

---

## 4. Nvidia says AI infrastructure demand remains strong
Nvidia signaled continued growth in AI infrastructure spending, as demand for chips and data-center buildout remains elevated. This remains one of the biggest macro stories in AI.  
**Sources:** Reuters, CNBC, company commentary  
- Reuters business/technology coverage ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/))

---

## 5. Governments continue pushing AI regulation and safety oversight
AI policy remains a major headline area, with governments in the U.S., EU, UK, and elsewhere continuing to debate rules around foundation models, copyright, transparency, and safety.  
**Sources:** Reuters, Financial Times, EU policy reporting  
- Reuters AI policy coverage ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/))

---

## 6. AI agents and enterprise copilots are becoming the new battleground
A major ongoing theme in today’s AI news is the race to build “AI agents” that can take actions, not just answer questions. Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Salesforce are all pushing this direction.  
**Sources:** Reuters, The Information, company blogs  
- Reuters AI section ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/))

---

## 7. Copyright and training-data lawsuits remain front-page AI news
Publishers, authors, artists, and media firms continue pressing legal cases over whether AI companies can train on copyrighted material without permission.  
**Sources:** Reuters, The New York Times, court reporting  
- Reuters legal/AI coverage ([reuters.com](https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/))

---

If you want, I can also give you:
1. a **real-time AI news roundup from the last 24 hours**,  
2. a **market-focused AI headlines list**, or  
3. a **company-specific roundup** for OpenAI, Google, Meta, Microsoft, and Anthropic.
